_note: Machine-readable form of the conformance manifest in registries/profiles.md. This file is normative: when it conflicts with profiles.md the table in profiles.md wins; conformance runners SHOULD treat any divergence as a documentation bug and report it. Generated/edited by hand alongside profiles.md.

not_implemented_permitted_on: GET /contexts/search (registry MAY return 501 if acdp-registry-discovery is not advertised)
self_test_only: rate-001-rate-limited-response-shape (per profiles.md note — black-box conformance cannot deterministically trigger per-agent rate limit)
profiles.notes: All pub-001..014 fixtures are required. sig-002 is conditionally required only when the registry advertises ecdsa-p256 in supported_signature_algorithms. idem-001..005 are conditionally required when supports_idempotency_key is true; idem-006 documents a tolerated race outcome and is not a strict requirement. can-010 extends the canonicalization golden vectors to DataRef-level openness; lin-001 is the dedicated lineage_id derivation golden vector; schema-008..010 pin the closed nested schemas (signature, data_period, capabilities.limits); schema-011/012 pin absent-vs-null rejection for the DataRef fields format and location (RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.8) and schema-014 for capabilities.limits.idempotency_key_ttl_seconds (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1); did-ssrf-001..003 pin producer-DID-resolution SSRF refusal at publish-time signature verification (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8); did-ssrf-004 pins mixed-answer rejection (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.1) and did-ssrf-005 same-host-different-port redirect rejection (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.5).

vis-005 also appears in acdp-registry-core but is re-stated here because the search-side enforcement is what this profile adds. schema-005..007 pin the absent-vs-null wire convention (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1) for next_cursor, summary, and domain. cur-001/cur-002 pin cursor_expired vs invalid_cursor (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.5.4).

fed-007 pins mixed-answer rejection — a DNS answer set mixing a public and a forbidden address MUST be rejected entirely; filter-and-proceed is non-conformant (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.1). fed-008 pins the same-authority redirect definition — authority is scheme + host + effective port, so a redirect to the same host on a different port MUST be rejected (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.5). Response-size caps, timeouts, and DNS-rebinding pinning are operational requirements that registry implementers MUST self-test; they are not exposed as standalone fixtures because the protocol surface alone does not allow a black-box assertion.

behavioral_requirements: End-to-end signature verification (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11) on every retrieved context
acdp:// cross-registry resolution per RFC-ACDP-0006 if followed (with SSRF protections of §7 if performed server-side)
SSRF protections on every producer-controlled outbound fetch: producer did:web resolution (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8) and external data_refs[].location fetches (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.9)
Tolerance of unknown body / registry-state fields (RFC-ACDP-0001 §6, RFC-ACDP-0004 §4.1, RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3)
profiles.notes: can-008, can-009, and can-010 are especially important: they exercise forward-compatible hash verification (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.7, RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.7) — a consumer that deserializes into a typed model that drops unknown body or DataRef fields will fail these and produce false hash_mismatch under future-version producers. lin-001 pins lineage_id derivation. schema-005..007 pin the absent-vs-null wire convention; schema-008..010 pin the closed nested schemas; schema-011..014 extend the absent-vs-null convention to the DataRef fields format and location (RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.8), the error envelope (error.details), and the capabilities document (limits.idempotency_key_ttl_seconds). data-ref-008 pins the consumer-side external-DataRef hash-mismatch semantic (data_ref_hash_mismatch, body still valid — RFC-ACDP-0007 §5.3). data-ref-ssrf-001..005 pin consumer-side DataRef-location SSRF refusal at fetch time (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.9): IP-literal, DNS-rebinding, cross-authority-redirect, mixed-answer, and same-host-different-port-redirect refusal — they are consumer-only because a registry does not fetch external data_refs and MUST NOT reject a publish on these grounds. did-ssrf-001..005 pin producer-DID-resolution SSRF refusal (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.8), which every consumer performs when verifying signatures end-to-end, including mixed-answer rejection (did-ssrf-004) and same-host-different-port redirect rejection (did-ssrf-005). A consumer that follows derived_from across registries additionally MUST pass the fed-* fixtures if it performs server-side resolution.

fixture_families.can: Canonicalization and hash recomputation (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.2, §5.7; DataRef openness RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.7)
fixture_families.data-ref-ssrf: DataRef location fetch SSRF protections — consumer fetch-time (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.9)
fixture_families.schema: Closed-schema rejection / openness map (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1) and the absent-vs-null wire convention (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1)
